title: Favourite Song
wikitext:
[]: Favourite Song?             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Jun 23, 2005 3:25 pm By "song" I mean only ones with lyrics, duh. So which is your favourite, out of the entire series? I remember watching Eva the first time, and the opening had no subtitles, so I didn't have a clue what the lyrics of Cruel Angel's Thesis were about. But I was amazed that a song in a completely different language could have so much impact. It was amazing. And let's not forget how well the animation is timed to the music, that really adds to it. Or Come, Sweet Death . When it starts in EoE is one of my all-time favourite moments out of any film. A few moments of silence, then that haunting piano opening. And it just seems to fit so well with what's going on; it's freaky. And no one forgets that immortal image; the moon rising in the background, the sillohoutte of Rei slowly spinning, and Fly me to the moon playing. So, out of all the songs in the series, which one do you think is the best/is used the best way? Personally, I would say Come, Sweet Death .

Thats my story and im sticking to it!

Article: Favourite Song
[]: Favourite Song?             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Jun 23, 2005 3:25 pm By "song" I mean only ones with lyrics, duh. So which is your favourite, out of the entire series? I remember watching Eva the first time, and the opening had no subtitles, so I didn't have a clue what the lyrics of Cruel Angel's Thesis were about. But I was amazed that a song in a completely different language could have so much impact. It was amazing. And let's not forget how well the animation is timed to the music, that really adds to it. Or Come, Sweet Death . When it starts in EoE is one of my all-time favourite moments out of any film. A few moments of silence, then that haunting piano opening. And it just seems to fit so well with what's going on; it's freaky. And no one forgets that immortal image; the moon rising in the background, the sillohoutte of Rei slowly spinning, and Fly me to the moon playing. So, out of all the songs in the series, which one do you think is the best/is used the best way? Personally, I would say Come, Sweet Death .
